label: Vanehouse Passage
description: Vanehouse Passage forms the narrow interior run beneath the Prismwake’s refractive vane lattice, where the ship’s heading bands are carried as disciplined lines of copper-white light across matte black bulkheads. Gasketed seams and ribbed decking are engineered to trap electrostatically charged glass dust before it reaches adjacent compartments, with drainage channels leading to sealed scupper cisterns. Grounded conductor-strips run the length of the corridor, tying the structure to the rails’ discharge system and keeping the air dry and faintly ozonated. Low inspection alcoves and recessed tool niches emphasize maintenance over ornament, reflecting the vessel’s twilight-sea design doctrine.
